items.title: Extract shared ingestion core
items.description: Create unified ingestion system for inbox + input hub to prevent code duplication
items.notes: Prevents duplication between inbox and input hub systems. Both systems need validation, classification, routing, and deduplication. Extract common patterns into shared core that both can use.
benefits: Single source of truth for ingestion logic
Consistent behavior across all input types
items.approach: Extract validator, classifier, router into shared core. Both inbox and input hub become thin wrappers that call shared logic with input-type-specific handlers.

items.title: Refactor existing patterns for 8825+Goose integration
items.description: Integrate two-layer automation pattern (8825 brain + Goose execution) into existing workflow patterns rather than creating parallel system
items.notes: AI sweep identified 14 touchpoints and 4 critical pattern conflicts. Recommendation is to refactor existing patterns rather than add new parallel system. This preserves 8825 as brain/context layer and uses Goose as execution layer via MCP.

ai_analysis.recommendation: Refactor existing patterns instead of adding new one

benefits: Unified automation approach across all workflows
Uses Goose as execution layer via MCP
Maintains consistency with existing critical patterns
items.approach: Review and refactor existing workflow patterns (TGIF auto-route, achievement detection, MCP servers) to support 8825→Goose handoff pattern. Ensure task specs from 8825 can be executed by Goose via MCP without creating parallel systems.
use_cases: Scanned letter → OCR → 8825 routing → deadline extraction → Goose creates calendar events
Achievement detection → 8825 context → Goose executes mining
TGIF notes → 8825 routing → Goose handles distribution
questions: Which existing patterns need refactoring first?
What's the right abstraction boundary between 8825 (brain) and Goose (execution)?
How do we maintain backward compatibility with existing workflows?

items.title: Partner Credit System - Automation & Integration
items.description: Automate credit tracking and integrate with 8825 task history, validation protocol, and metrics dashboard
items.notes: Phase 2 of partner credit system. Phase 1 (manual tracking) must complete 90-day pilot successfully before building automation.
benefits: Link work orders to 8825 task history
Feed actual hours to time estimator
items.approach: Build 8825 integration layer with auto-logging to ledger, validation protocol automation, metrics dashboard, and automation catalog. Integrate with existing task tracking and time estimation systems.
deliverables: Metrics dashboard (balance, WOs, cycle time, SLA)
